draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Runners respond better to a friendly, motivational tone than a clinical, data-focused voice
facade.label: Casual and encouraging
facade.acceptImplies: User values personality and emotional connection in the app—wants it to feel like a supportive friend, not a fitness computer
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers straightforward, no-nonsense communication—voice should be neutral and efficient, focused on data delivery

facade.hypothesis: Runners respond better to warm color temperatures and soft lighting that feels encouraging rather than clinical.
facade.label: Glowing warmth
facade.acceptImplies: Weather data presented with warm oranges, ambers, and soft glows; encouraging, sunrise-like aesthetic that motivates early morning runs.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers cooler, crisper visual language—blues, grays, stark contrast—that feels more objective and performance-focused.

evidence.hypothesis: Runners respond better to a friendly, motivational tone than a clinical, data-focused voice
evidence.implication: User values personality and emotional connection in the app—wants it to feel like a supportive friend, not a fitness computer

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer clean, minimal iconography over photorealistic or skeuomorphic visual language
facade.label: Bright flat icons
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into simple, single-color or 2-color icon sets; avoid photography or 3D renders; consider playful but flat illustrations
facade.rejectImplies: Explore richer visual language: gradients, shadows, depth, photographic elements, or hand-drawn textures for data visualization and UI controls

facade.hypothesis: Runners want persistent access to current pace, distance, and time without interrupting map view
facade.axisTargeted: Layout structure: persistent sidebar vs full-screen immersion
facade.label: Sidebar with run stats
facade.acceptImplies: User values information density and constant metric visibility during active runs; sidebar pattern fits runner workflow
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers minimalist, distraction-free map focus; metrics should hide or appear only on demand; full-screen immersion matters more than always-on data

facade.hypothesis: Runners will find real-time speed metrics (pace/min per mile) more actionable than weather alone, creating a dual-focus interface that layers performance data with conditions
facade.axisTargeted: information hierarchy: isolated weather cards vs. integrated runner metrics
facade.label: Live pace overlay
facade.acceptImplies: Users want performance context baked into weather—speed, cadence, or distance goals visible at a glance alongside temp/wind. Suggests tight coupling of athlete + environment data.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want clean separation between conditions and performance—check weather first, then run with a separate app or mental model. Suggests weather should be pure, uncluttered, and standalone.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Runners want real-time speed feedback integrated spatially—brief animated pulses at current location show if they're hitting target pace, reducing cognitive load vs glancing at numbers
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior—animated feedback vs static readouts; gesture-driven interaction vs display-only
facade.label: Pace pulses on map
facade.acceptImplies: User values glanceable, motion-based cues over numeric precision; animated location-tied feedback fits their mental model; likely favors fluid, continuous state updates
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers stable, persistent numeric display; finds animation distracting during exertion; wants explicit control over when/how pace data appears; may favor snappy, minimal transitions

facade.hypothesis: Runners prioritize clear, glanceable weather metrics over personality when making split-second go/no-go decisions
facade.axisTargeted: tone and information hierarchy — personality-driven vs data-efficient
facade.label: No-nonsense data
facade.acceptImplies: User values efficiency and legibility over emotional connection; wants minimal friction between question (Can I run?) and answer (Temperature, wind, rain %).
facade.rejectImplies: User confirms personality and warmth matter more than stripped-down clarity; hesitant accept on 'Casual and encouraging' wasn't just politeness—emotional scaffolding is core to their app experience.

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind impact, and run-friendly forecasts at a glance.
draft.html:
RunWeather

San Francisco, CA

Partly Cloudy

Humidity
65%
Feels Like

UV Index
4

Hourly Forecast

Now

2 PM
64°
4 PM
61°
6 PM
58°
8 PM
55°

Run Score

8.2/10
